text: brows lowered and his jaw tightened . “No. I mean, yeah, I know. But, I need to go .” She looked at the floor, cheeks heating, and if she had to explain human bodily functions to a damned bot she’d lose it . “Where do you think you’ll get to ?” “God dammit, I have to take a piss !” Ronin stared, blinking once . “If I have to spell it out for you any more clearly — ” She snapped her mouth shut when he raised a hand and pointed into the hallway . “What’s down there ?” “ Another room .” “So you expect me to go in the corner, or, what? Out the window?” Her embarrassment was rapidly shifting to irritation . “Follow,” he said, and despite her annoyance, she did . He only took a few steps, stopping before the first door to the right. She stared at him, arms crossed. Frowning, he opened the door, turned on the light, and moved aside . Lara leaned forward and looked into the room . It was much different from the others she’d seen. For one, it was far smaller, and the floor was tiled. There was a large white tub along the far wall, and a wash basin beneath a mirror. She’d seen people use such things back home, usually as planters for their meager crop allotments. Between the two was another porcelain object, about knee-high and bowl-shaped, that reminded her of the outhouses the humans used . They had things like it at Kitty’s, but Lara had never been comfortable enough to use them . “ So …” “I don’t have any personal experience, but I think you sit on the toilet,” Ronin said, and if there wasn’t dry amusement in his voice, she was crazy . “Don’t you dare make fun of me!” She glared at him and gestured at the porcelain seat. “You mean that thing ?” He nodded . Lara entered the room, shuddering at the chill of the tiles against her bare feet. Stopping in front of the toilet , she lifted the lid. The inside was halfway filled with water . “ In … this ?” “Yes. And you push down the handle when you’re done .” She tapped the silver handle on the box-shaped rear portion of the toilet. When it didn’t do anything, she pressed harder, and watched in startled amazement as the water inside the bowl swirled and vanished into the hole at the bottom. Then, on its own, the bowl refilled . “Did you see that?” She swung her gaze back to Ronin . “Maybe I’d best leave you to it,” he said. “If you turn the knobs in the tub, water will come out. Right for cold, left for hot. Clean yourself up. I’ll leave something dry for you to wear in the hallway .” He left, closing the door behind him, and Lara returned her attention to the toilet. After she examined it from top to bottom, she relieved herself. Her guilt in cleaning herself with some of the paper from the nearby roll was understandable; paper was so rare amongst humans that they’d never dream of using it in such a fashion. She watched the soiled water disappear with no less amazement than the first time . That done, she removed her wraps, untying the knots and dropping the wet cloths onto the floor. They fell with a heavy splat. She felt lighter without them, though no warmer. Her legs were caked with mud from the walk, and the tiles were smudged with her dirty footprints. She’d wash her legs and feet to avoid making any more of a mess and call it good . She stepped into the tub, running a hand over the strangely-textured curtain bunched to one side, and turned to examine the metal knobs on the wall. She’d rarely had the luxury of bathing; there was only one water pump for everyone to share, and Warlord often cut it off at his whim. It was far more important to keep people and crops watered than to be clean . Tilting her head, she turned the left knob. Water flowed from the spigot and pooled around her feet, immediately cloudy with grime. To her wonderment, it warmed. She cupped her hands and splashed her chilled body . Soon, the warmth became heat. It was quickly too much for her to handle. She turned the middle knob, hoping for some cool water to balance the flow. Water sprayed onto her from above, first ice cold, then scalding hot . Lara screamed and stumbled back. One of her flailing hands caught on the curtain, and she used it to pull herself over the edge of the tub. Her feet slipped the instant they touched the floor. She landed hard on her backside with another cry, legs sprawled . “Fuck!” Pain radiated from her ass and up her side . The door burst open and Ronin was suddenly in the room . Lara looked up at him with rounded eyes. There was a small gun in his right hand, its dull black metal absorbing the overhead light. He held a waded-up piece of cloth in his other hand. Their eyes met . “Are you all—” His gaze dipped . “Get out!” Lara screamed, crossing her arms over her chest pressing her legs together, knees raised . As though the situation were routine, he shifted his attention to the tub. Steam gathered at the ceiling. Random drops of water landed on Lara’s back. He placed the cloth — a shirt, she realized — next to the wash basin and stepped over her, reaching up to draw the curtain closed . She watched, paralyzed by anger, embarrassment, and fear, as he held a hand in the water for several seconds. Then he reached in and turned one of the knobs . “Should be fine now. Soap is in the corner.” He didn’t look at her as
